title: Zeff Explains the Baratie’s Harsh Rules
description: On pages 17–20, the crew reaches the floating restaurant Baratie, where Luffy’s impulsiveness, Fullbody’s arrogance, and Sanji’s sharp code collide.
scene: The Baratie is loud, violent, and rule-bound: cooks fight like pirates, customers cause trouble, and Sanji’s character begins to emerge through conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I'm only going to say this one more time so listen up!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I'm a customer, and as such, you'll bring me food this instant!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: So you don't have any money?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Damn that Patty, how dare he break the table!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: If you can't pay the bill,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Show that pirate who's boss, Patty!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Oh? Is that your stomach rumbling?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: That was... just me farting, you bastard.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: So hurry up... and bring me some food.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: If you're not a paying customer, then get the hell outta here!

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: This is a restaurant for paying customers!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I ain't even gonna give you a single bread crumb to a broke-ass pirate scum like you!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I don't want to" involve myself with this restaurant anymore!

summary: This part introduces the Baratie as more than a restaurant; it is a battlefield of pride, hunger, and harsh principles that will define Sanji’s arc.
